iti30:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti30:2.1: “Monks, there are these two things that cause remorse.
iti30:2.3:
There is the case of the person who has not done what is admirable, has not done what is skillful,
has not given protection to those in fear, and instead has done what is evil, savage, & cruel.
iti30:2.4:
Thinking,
‘I have not done what is admirable,’ he feels remorse.
Thinking,
‘I have done what is evil,’ he feels remorse.
iti30:2.5: These are the two things that cause remorse.”
iti30:4.1: not having done what is skillful,
iti30:4.2: having done much that is not,
iti30:4.3: at the break-up of the body, the undiscerning one reappears in
